title: Publications | Ministry of Health NZ
content:
## Publications Ngā whakaputanga kōrero
- Food and Nutrition Guidelines for Healthy Children and Young People (Aged 2–18 years): A background paper Publication date: 16 February 2015 This background paper provides evidence-based technical information and best practice recommendations on nutrition and physical activity for health practitioners working with children and young people. Publication Alcohol Long-term conditions Child health Youth health Mental health Asian health Māori health Pacific health Oral health Physical activity Nutrition

## Food and Nutrition Guidelines for Healthy Children and Young People (Aged 2–18 years): A background paper
This background paper provides evidence-based technical information and best practice recommendations on nutrition and physical activity for health practitioners working with children and young people.
- Long-term conditions
- Specialist Opioid Substitution Treatment (OST) Service Audit and Review Tool Publication date: 8 December 2014 This document sets out the audit requirements to determine whether practices within specialist OST services are consistent with current best practice in OST and to clarify service providers’ responsibilities in providing OST. Publication Drugs

## Specialist Opioid Substitution Treatment (OST) Service Audit and Review Tool
This document sets out the audit requirements to determine whether practices within specialist OST services are consistent with current best practice in OST and to clarify service providers’ responsibilities in providing OST.
- Tobacco Use 2012/13: New Zealand Health Survey Publication date: 4 December 2014 This publication shows how New Zealand is progressing towards being smokefree in 2025 by providing a snapshot of tobacco use in New Zealand, using data from the 2012/13 New Zealand Health Survey. Publication Tobacco Statistics Surveys

## Tobacco Use 2012/13: New Zealand Health Survey
This publication shows how New Zealand is progressing towards being smokefree in 2025 by providing a snapshot of tobacco use in New Zealand, using data from the 2012/13 New Zealand Health Survey.
- Problem Substance Use Among New Zealand Secondary Schools Students Publication date: 26 November 2014 This report explores the health issues and contexts of New Zealand secondary school students who use substances at very high levels. It provides evidence and awareness of the problems that are connected to substance use. Publication Addiction Youth health

## Problem Substance Use Among New Zealand Secondary Schools Students
This report explores the health issues and contexts of New Zealand secondary school students who use substances at very high levels. It provides evidence and awareness of the problems that are connected to substance use.
- Audit Report Writing Guide Publication date: 12 November 2014 This Audit Report Writing Guide offers guidance to designated auditing agency auditors on preparing audit reports for the Ministry of Health so that each report provides clear, consistent and helpful information. Publication Certification of health care services

## Audit Report Writing Guide
This Audit Report Writing Guide offers guidance to designated auditing agency auditors on preparing audit reports for the Ministry of Health so that each report provides clear, consistent and helpful information.
- Certification of health care services
- Improving the lives of people with dementia Publication date: 10 September 2014 This publication outlines nine key areas the Ministry of Health will support over the next three years to maximize the health, independence and wellbeing of people with dementia. Publication Older people (65+)

## Improving the lives of people with dementia
This publication outlines nine key areas the Ministry of Health will support over the next three years to maximize the health, independence and wellbeing of people with dementia.
- ’Ala Mo’ui : Pathways to Pacific Health and Wellbeing 2014–2018 Publication date: 26 June 2014 This national plan sets out priority outcomes and actions to improve health outcomes for Pacific peoples. The plan will contribute to the overall vision of health equity for Pacific peoples in New Zealand. Publication Pacific health

## ’Ala Mo’ui : Pathways to Pacific Health and Wellbeing 2014–2018
This national plan sets out priority outcomes and actions to improve health outcomes for Pacific peoples. The plan will contribute to the overall vision of health equity for Pacific peoples in New Zealand.
- Equity of Health Care for Māori: A framework Publication date: 20 June 2014 The framework guides health practitioners, health organisations and the health system to achieve equitable health care for Māori through leadership, knowledge and commitment. Publication Inequalities Māori health

## Equity of Health Care for Māori: A framework
The framework guides health practitioners, health organisations and the health system to achieve equitable health care for Māori through leadership, knowledge and commitment.
- The Guide to He Korowai Oranga – Māori Health Strategy Publication date: 20 June 2014 This short guide provides a summary of the He Korowai Oranga – Māori Health Strategy. This includes ‘ Pae Ora ’ and its 3 elements and how the existing framework has been strengthened for the future. Publication Inequalities Māori health

## The Guide to He Korowai Oranga – Māori Health Strategy
This short guide provides a summary of the He Korowai Oranga – Māori Health Strategy. This includes ‘ Pae Ora ’ and its 3 elements and how the existing framework has been strengthened for the future.
- Youth Gambling: The health and wellbeing of New Zealand secondary school students in 2012 Publication date: 16 June 2014 This survey is part of series of larger surveys that aim to provide information on the health and wellbeing of young people attending New Zealand secondary schools. The current survey was conducted in 2012 and involved questions about gambling, amongst other topics. Publication Gambling research Youth health Statistics Gambling harm

## Youth Gambling: The health and wellbeing of New Zealand secondary school students in 2012
This survey is part of series of larger surveys that aim to provide information on the health and wellbeing of young people attending New Zealand secondary schools. The current survey was conducted in 2012 and involved questions about gambling, amongst other topics.
- Like Minds, Like Mine National Plan 2014-2019 Publication date: 29 May 2014 Like Minds, Like Mine is a programme to increase social inclusion and reduce stigma and discrimination for people with experience of mental illness. Publication Mental health

## Like Minds, Like Mine National Plan 2014-2019
Like Minds, Like Mine is a programme to increase social inclusion and reduce stigma and discrimination for people with experience of mental illness.
- Transition Planning Guidelines for Infant, Child and Adolescent Mental Health / Alcohol and Other Drugs Services 2014 Publication date: 29 May 2014 This guideline is for DHB funded infant, child and adolescent mental health, and youth-focused alcohol and drug services. It assists them to develop and implement planning processes for young people who are transitioning from their services. Publication Alcohol Drugs Child health Youth health Mental health

## Transition Planning Guidelines for Infant, Child and Adolescent Mental Health / Alcohol and Other Drugs Services 2014
This guideline is for DHB funded infant, child and adolescent mental health, and youth-focused alcohol and drug services. It assists them to develop and implement planning processes for young people who are transitioning from their services.
- Te Ohonga Ake: The Determinants of Health for Māori Children and Young People Publication date: 23 May 2014 This report explores the determinants of Māori infants, children and young people using a range of routinely collected data sources. Publication Babies and toddlers (0–2 years) Youth health Māori health

## Te Ohonga Ake: The Determinants of Health for Māori Children and Young People
This report explores the determinants of Māori infants, children and young people using a range of routinely collected data sources.
- Babies and toddlers (0–2 years)
- Tikanga ā-Rongoā Publication date: 23 May 2014 The Tikanga ā-Rongoā standard is to ensure the safe delivery of quality rongoā services. This voluntary standard helps. Publication Māori health
The Tikanga ā-Rongoā standard is to ensure the safe delivery of quality rongoā services. This voluntary standard helps.
- Enrolling Babies at Birth Publication date: 2 May 2014 This resource provides general practice with practical suggestions to support the process of ensuring newborns are enrolled with general practice as close to birth as possible and no later than two weeks after birth. Publication Babies and toddlers (0–2 years) Primary health care

## Enrolling Babies at Birth
This resource provides general practice with practical suggestions to support the process of ensuring newborns are enrolled with general practice as close to birth as possible and no later than two weeks after birth.
